title: Agentic Web Default Stack
summary: A conservative stack-routing policy for generated web products that keeps boring defaults unless the domain clearly requires a different runtime.

retrieval.description: Use when selecting the default technology path for generated apps, especially when the user has not requested a specific stack.

donorSources.label: Vite
donorSources.notes: Default fast frontend runtime for generated SPAs and prototypes.

donorSources.label: TanStack Query
donorSources.notes: Default server-state layer for generated frontend clients.

donorSources.label: Hono
donorSources.notes: Default web-standard API layer for edge/serverless-friendly modules.

donorSources.label: Neon
donorSources.notes: Postgres with branching and serverless ergonomics.

prompt: Default to Vite + React for generated product surfaces unless SEO/public SSR or a full-stack framework is required.
Use TanStack Query for server state and Hono or Fastify for API surfaces.
Use Postgres-shaped relational persistence with Drizzle or Kysely when operational state matters.
Choose specialized runtimes only when the product shape demands them: game engine for games, Astro for content-heavy sites, Next.js for public SSR/SEO or one-framework delivery.
requiredElements: Explicit reason for any non-default stack choice
Runtime scripts for dev, build, start, verify, and smoke when applicable
Clear API/client boundary and data ownership model
Dependency list kept minimal and justified
forbiddenPatterns: choosing Next.js by reflex for every frontend
using Firebase or Convex without realtime/offline collaboration need
adding a heavy backend framework for a small API
mixing SSR framework constraints into game runtimes
allowedSubstitutions: Use Next.js when SEO, public SSR, app router conventions, or a single deployable full-stack app are explicit requirements.
Use Convex when realtime collaboration is the product differentiator.
Use Turso/libSQL for edge/local-first/offline SQLite-shaped requirements.
Use Prisma when CRUD DX is more important than SQL-first control.
stackHints: Baseline: Vite + React + TanStack Query + TanStack Router + Hono + Drizzle + Postgres.
Games should use Vite plus Phaser/Pixi/Three/PlayCanvas rather than SSR.
WhatsApp/CRM systems should prefer Postgres, TanStack Query, and an explicit webhook/idempotency layer.

validators.description: Generated workspaces should explain any departure from the default stack path.
validators.evidence: README, module manifest, or build dossier stack-decision note.

validators.description: Runnable modules must expose verify/dev/build/start/smoke paths as appropriate.
validators.evidence: package.json scripts and smoke test logs.

validators.description: Dependencies should map to product requirements and not appear as unchecked generator defaults.
validators.evidence: Dependency manifest and generated README notes.
